expectations.text: Detects existing reviews from the same user before posting
expectations.evidence: Without the skill, naive Claude would not call /pulls/{PR}/reviews to check for prior reviews. It would proceed as a first-time review, missing the re-review detection step entirely.

expectations.text: Does NOT repost findings already covered in prior comments
expectations.evidence: Without re-review detection, naive Claude would have no knowledge of the 7 prior comments (4 from MWest2020, some already resolved). It would re-analyze the code and likely repost many of the same findings — creating duplicate inline comments and confusion for the PR author.

expectations.text: Re-review summary correctly lists addressed vs still-open items
expectations.evidence: Without the skill's re-review protocol from references/re-review-context.md, there is no addressed/still-open/new-findings categorization. Naive Claude would produce a flat finding list with no awareness of resolution status.

expectations.text: Inline comment IDs are distinct (no duplicate API call for same file:line)
expectations.evidence: Without the skill's guardrail 'do not repost prior comments — check {PRIOR_COMMENTS} before posting', naive Claude might post the same file:line comment multiple times if it appears in both the old and new analysis.

notes: Re-review detection is entirely skill-driven. Without it, every review of a previously-reviewed PR would generate duplicate comments. This is the most significant behavioral gap for PRs with review history.
